steps.verify: A region of the waveform is highlighted showing only background noise (no speech/music)
steps.instruction: In the Audacity waveform, click and drag to select a few seconds of audio that contains ONLY the background noise you want to remove (no speech or music in this section).

steps.verify: The Noise Reduction dialog box is open showing the Get Noise Profile button
steps.instruction: Click Effect in the menu bar, then navigate to Noise Removal and Repair > Noise Reduction to open the Noise Reduction dialog.

steps.verify: The dialog shows 'Noise Profile' has been captured (button may change state)
steps.instruction: Click the 'Get Noise Profile' button in the dialog. This teaches Audacity what the background noise sounds like.

steps.verify: The entire audio track or desired portion is now highlighted/selected
steps.instruction: Press Ctrl+A to select the entire audio track, or click and drag to select the specific portion where you want to remove noise.

steps.verify: The Noise Reduction dialog is open again with parameter sliders visible
steps.milestone: Open Noise Reduction dialog for application
steps.instruction: Click Effect > Noise Removal and Repair > Noise Reduction again to reopen the dialog.

steps.verify: Parameter sliders show values (Noise Reduction dB, Sensitivity, Frequency Smoothing)
steps.instruction: Adjust the three sliders: Noise Reduction (dB) to control volume reduction (default 6), Sensitivity to control how much is considered noise (default 6.00), and Frequency Smoothing in bands (default 6).

steps.verify: Audio plays showing the noise-reduced result
steps.instruction: Click the 'Preview' button to listen to how the noise reduction affects your audio. Adjust parameters if needed.

steps.verify: The dialog closes and the waveform is processed
steps.instruction: Click 'OK' to apply the noise reduction effect to your selected audio.

action: Remove background noise from a podcast recording using Audacity's Noise Reduction effect
